functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_statistcs.compute_area_from_4326
documentation:
Shapely don't care about the unity used in polygons. EPSG:4326 polygons
coordinates are in decimal degrees values whereas the area shall be
expressed in meter. The simplest and most accurate method to manage the
geographic to geodesic coordinates is to use pyproj library to map
the polygon on a geoid (i.e. WGS84)
\:param geometry:
\:return:

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_statistcs._compute_simplify
documentation:
Compute the simplification of the passed geometry and set the tolerance
statistic info.
\:param geometry: geometry to simplify
\:param tolerance: the expected tolerance

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_statistcs._compute_convex_hull
documentation:
Compute the convex hull of the passed geometry and statistic info.
\:param geometry: geometry to simplify

methods.name: footprint_facility.footprint_statistcs.FootprintStatistics._compute_data_frames
documentation:
Performs the geometric comparison ot this statistics inputs.
The computation uses GeoPanda.
\:return: the set of GeoPanda geometries, ordered as followed:

> 1. input origin_footprint
> 2. input optimized_footprint,
> 3. footprint showing the added part by the optimization
> 4. footprint showing the removed part by the optimization

methods.name: footprint_facility.footprint_statistcs.FootprintStatistics.map
documentation:
Generates follium/leaflet map to display the results
\:return: the follium map that can be save into html

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility.check_cross_antimeridian
documentation:
Checks if the geometry pass over ±180 longitude position.
The detection of antimeridian is performed according to the distance
between longitudes positions between consecutive points of the geometry
points. The distance shall be greater than 180 to avoid revert longitude
signs around greenwich meridian 0.
It is also considered crossing antimeridian when one of the polygon
longitude is exactly to +-180 degrees.
\:parameter geometry: The geometry to be controlled.
\:return: True if the geometry pass over antimeridian, False otherwise.

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._check_contains_north_pole
documentation:
Check if the given geometry contains North Pole.
Warning: the re-projection process does not work properly when coordinates
of the geometry pass over antimeridian. This method cannot be used without
applying the polar inclusive method as implemented in
`rework_to_polygon_geometry`.

See comment on globals variable for projection details.

```
parameter geometry

the complex reference geometry.

return

True if the given geometry contains North Pole
```

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._check_contains_south_pole
documentation:
Check if the given geometry contains South Pole.
Warning: the re-projection process does not work properly when coordinates
of the geometry pass over antimeridian. This method cannot be used without
applying the polar inclusive method as implemented in
`rework_to_polygon_geometry`.

See comment on globals variable for projection details.

```
parameter geometry

the complex reference geometry.

return

True if the given geometry contains South Pole
```

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._check_contains_pole
documentation:
Checks if the geometry pass over the North or South Pole.
WARN: this method shall be used only once improved geometry with inclusion
of polar point.
\:parameter geometry: the geometry to be controlled.
\:return: True if the geometry contains polar point, False otherwise.

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._plus360
documentation:
Translate to +360 degrees the x longitude value when value is negative.

Note: The translation is only applicable to longitude values with unit
in degrees. It shall be efficient when previous coordinate longitude is
180 degrees far from this point.

```
param x

the longitude to be translated

return

the shifted longitude when required.
```

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._polynom_coefficients
documentation:
Resolves the linear equation passing by given p1 and p2 coordinates.
\:return: Two values: first is the leading coefficient (m) second is the
constant coefficient (b) that can be used as Y=m.X+b

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._lat_cross_antimeridian
documentation:
Retrieves the latitude position in the line drawn by 2
point parameters p1 and p2 and crossing ±180 longitude.

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._split_polygon_to_antimeridian
documentation:
This method splits geometry among the antimeridan area. It removes link
to the pole if any.
\:param geometry: the geometry to split
\:return: polygon or multipolygon if the geometry requires to be split.

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._split_polygon_to_equator
documentation:
Split geometry among the equator: this is useful when the footprint cover
both hemisphere and includes overlaping with antimeridian: In this case
both poles are included into the shape of the footprint and intersection
method fails.
\:param geometry:
\:return:

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility._rework_to_polygon_geometry
documentation:
Rework the geometry to manage polar and antimeridian singularity.
This process implements the **Polar inclusive algorithm**.
The objective of this algorithm is to add the North/South Pole into
the list of coordinates of geometry polygon at the antimeridian cross.

When the geometry contains the pole the single polygon geometry including
the pole in its border point list is properly interpreted by displays
systems. When the geometry does not contain the pole, the geometry shall be
split among the antimeridian line.

```
param geometry

the geometry crossing the antimeridian.

return

the modified geometry with the closest pole included at
antimeridian crossing.
```

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility.rework_to_linestring_geometry
documentation:
Elaborates linestring geometry from thin polygon and manage the
antimeridian cross.

```
param geometry



return

```

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility.cartesian_to_geodetic
documentation:
Convert polygon expressed in meter into latlon geometry.
\:param geometry: the metric expressed geometry
\:return: the latlon geometry

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility.geodetic_to_cartesian
documentation:
Convert latlon polygon into metric coordinates.
\:param geometry: the latlong geometry
\:return: the metric geometry

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility.simplify
documentation:
Returns a simplified representation of the geometric object.
This method wraps shapely library <https://shapely.readthedocs.io/en/>

> stable/reference/shapely.simplify.html#shapely.simplify

All points in the simplified object will be within the tolerance distance
of the original geometry. default a slower algorithm is used that
preserves topology. If preserve topology is set to False the much quicker
Douglas-Peucker algorithm is used.

```
param geometry



param tolerance

The maximum allowed geometry displacement. The higher
```

this value, the smaller the number of vertices in the resulting geometry.
\:param preserve_topology: default (True), the operation will avoid
creating invalid geometries (checking for collapses, ring-intersections,
etc.), but this is computationally more expensive.
\:param tolerance_in_meter: Default tolerance unit is the geometry unit.

> The usage in the libraries, geometries are in latlon projection and
> coordinates are expressed in degrees. When setting meter for tolerance,
> the footprint is converted in meter to manage the Douglas-Pucker
> algorithm in meter based distances. Then, once processed, the geometry
> is back to latlon to be returned as result.

```
return

```

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility.to_wkt
documentation:
Convert the geometry to string WKT format
\:param geometry: the geometry to convert
\:return: the string in WKT format
Utilities for Geometry manipulation
- convert to wkt
- convert to geojson
- build sample disk footprint from its center and radius.
Create WKT string from Geometry

functions.name: footprint_facility.footprint_facility.to_geojson
documentation:
Convert the geometry to string GeoJSON format. The identifier of the
feature can be provided by the caller as well as the property dictionary.
\:param geometry: the geometry to convert
\:param feature_id: a user defined feature identifier, the identifier will
be automatically generated if not provided by the user.
\:param properties: a set of property to embed into the feature.
\:return: the GeoJSON string
Create GeoJSON string from Geometry

It does not intersect antimeridian nor pole.

Footprint with overlapping on the North Pole.It also passes other
both North and South Pole.

The fact the footprint cross both north and South Pole fails with de
manipulation and display.

This product is an old historical product and this use case has not
been retrieved in CDSE.

methods.name: footprint_facility.test_footprint_facility.TestFootprintFacility.test_S1A_WV_SLC__1SSV_no_antimeridian
documentation:
Manage imagette of Sentinel-1 wave mode.
This Test use real manifest.safe file of S1A WV data.
convex hull algortihm generates a polygon reducing points number
from 470 to 53.

methods.name: footprint_facility.test_footprint_facility.TestFootprintFacility.test_S1A_WV_SLC__1SSV_crossing_antimeridian
documentation:
Manage imagette of Sentinel-1 wave mode.
This Test use real manifest.safe file of S1A WV data. This data crosses
the antimridian.
Convex hull algortihm generates a polygon reducing points number
from 470 to 53. But This algorithm does not support antimeridian
singularity, it shall be split into 2 polygons before execution.
:return:

methods.name: footprint_facility.test_footprint_facility.TestFootprintFacility.test_jan_07_06_2024_S1
documentation: Issue reported by 07/06/2024, regarding product reported from https://datahub.creodias.eu/odata/v1 "S1A_EW_OCN__2SDH_20240602T183043_20240602T183154_054148_0695B3_" "DC42.SAFE" Command line used: #Sentinel-6 product="S1A_EW_OCN__2SDH_20240602T183043_20240602T183154_054148_" "0695B3_DC42.SAFE" wget -qO - 'https://datahub.creodias.eu/odata/v1/Products?' '$filter=((Name%20eq%20%27'$product'%27))' | jq '.value[] | .Footprint' | tr -d '"' | tr -d "'" | cut -f 2 -d ';' | xargs -I {} python3 -c ' from footprint_facility import to_wkt,rework_to_polygon_geometry; from shapely import wkt; print(to_wkt(rework_to_polygon_geometry(wkt.loads("{}"))));'
